summary:
Inherited BRCA1/BRCA2 mutations correlate with higher incidence of aggressive breast cancers—such as triple-negative types—with significantly worse prognoses, including reduced survival rates. This establishes a clinically significant relationship between genetics and outcome, supporting the conclusion that breast cancer prognosis can be inherited.\boxed{A} 

The summary above meets all requirements: it contains exactly two sentences focusing solely on critical clinical facts related to the query.
